message:
fix(membrane): use EWMA effective sample size for Bernstein bound in matrix concentration

The Bernstein concentration bound was computed using self.count (the total
number of observations ever seen) as the sample size n. However, the
spectral_deviation and matrix_var estimates are maintained via exponentially
weighted moving averages which inherently forget old data, giving them an
effective sample size that plateaus at approximately 1/alpha. Using the raw
unbounded count caused the bound to shrink toward zero as observations
accumulated, while the EWMA variance estimate remained at a constant floor,
leading to spurious threshold crossings.

Switch both observe() and bernstein_bound() to use self.effective_n -- the
EWMA-derived effective sample size -- so the bound correctly reflects the
statistical power of the estimator rather than falsely tightening.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
